query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity:

**Latest data (final week of June 2026):** Foreign investors remain heavy net **sellers** of KOSPI shares:
- **June 30, 2026:** Net sell of **₩3.8174 trillion** (KOSPI still rose +0.97% to 8,476.48 on domestic buying)
- **June 29, 2026:** Record single-day net sell of **₩7.7557 trillion**
- **Week ending June 28, 2026:** Net sell of **~₩16.64 trillion** (~$10.8bn)

**Broader trend:** This caps a record H1 2026 "Sell Korea" wave — **₩103.25 trillion** cumulative YTD net selling through June 1 (a record exceeding the 2008 crisis and 2020 COVID outflows), with May alone seeing ₩49tn net sold on KOSPI. The only interruption was a brief **₩1.7tn net buy on June 12** (first in 25 sessions).

**Context:** The KRX CEO frames it as profit-taking/rebalancing after KOSPI rose 76% in 2025 and ~108% YTD before the June 3 selloff. Selling is concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix amid AI-chip volatility. Notably, despite the selling, foreign ownership hit an all-time high of 35.3% (end-May) as prices rose.

The full structured findings, data points, and sources have been recorded.

key_facts: June 30, 2026: Foreign investors net sold ₩3.8174 trillion on KOSPI; index still rose +0.97% to close 8,476.48
June 29, 2026: Record single-day foreign net sell of ₩7.7557 trillion (Samsung Electronics ₩3.8675tn, SK Hynix ₩3.2981tn); KOSPI -0.20% to 8,394.65
Week ending June 28, 2026: Foreign investors net sold ~₩16.64 trillion (~$10.8 billion)
YTD through June 1, 2026: Record cumulative net selling of ₩103.2497 trillion on KOSPI, exceeding 2007-08 crisis (₩62tn) and 2020 COVID (₩25tn)
May 2026: Foreign monthly net sell of ₩47.019tn overall (KOSPI ₩49.041tn); Jan-May cumulative ~₩114.224tn over 5 straight months
June 12, 2026: Brief reversal — foreigners net bought ~₩1.7tn for first time in 25 trading days; KOSPI surged +8%, but selling soon resumed
June 23, 2026: KOSPI crashed -9.99% to 8,203.84 on massive foreign tech sell-offs; circuit breakers triggered
Foreign ownership hit all-time high of 35.3% at end-May 2026 (holdings ₩2,852.3tn) despite record selling, as prices rose
KRX CEO Jeong Eun-bo characterized the selloff as portfolio rebalancing/profit-taking after KOSPI rose 76% in 2025 and ~108% YTD, not a loss of confidence
Selling concentrated in semiconductor heavyweights Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix amid global AI-chip trade volatility
